gaps_addressed: GAP-4 Agent protocol ingestion (AG-UI / A2A / A2UI)

candidates.name: @prometheusags/frf-entity-management (Flint entity-management SDK)

evidence.claim: Flint already ships a RealtimeAdapter class with watchEntities(query): AsyncIterable<EntityEvent> and mutateEntity(record) — a clean entity-shaped seam that JSON-decodes the envelope payload and does NOT leak proto/tonic types.
EntityEvent shape is {entityType, entityId, tenantId, channelId, data, offset, correlationId} — maps near-1:1 to our EntityChange/ChangeSet contract.
frf-sdk is NOT published to npm (workspace:* dependency only); Flint is at Phase 0 with proto-v1 unfrozen.

risks: frf-sdk proto-v1 is unfrozen — depend on the entity-management facade (EntityEvent), never on generated proto types.
Not on npm — consume via local workspace link or a published preview; our published package must NOT hard-depend on it (peer/optional only).
offset is bigint — needs a checkpoint-store mapping to our adapter's resume semantics.
candidates.decision_rationale: GAP-1 is a bridge, not a from-scratch build. Build createFlintAdapter() in OUR repo that wraps the Flint RealtimeAdapter's AsyncIterable into our ChangeSet stream feeding RealtimeManager, against our stable RealtimeAdapter contract + the EntityEvent shim. frf-sdk stays an optional peer dep so proto churn never breaks our published build.

candidates.name: loro-crdt
candidates.fit_for_gap: GAP-2 CRDT / conflict resolution (reference impl behind MergeStrategy port)

evidence.claim: v1.13.5, MIT, updated 2026-06-21 (today) — actively maintained, past 1.0.
Loro leads crdt-benchmarks in every category except WASM load; Fugue algorithm (maximal non-interleaving); movable Tree CRDT + LWW Map fit entity graphs; 2-5x smaller encoding.
Rust core with JS bindings; List/LWW-Map/Tree/Text data types — LWW-Map maps directly onto our entity field model.

risks: WASM load cost on first use (mitigate: lazy dynamic import, only when a CRDT entity type is registered).
Youngest ecosystem (~12K weekly downloads) vs Yjs — fewer community bindings.
Flint's own CRDT engine choice (Loro vs Automerge) is undecided; aligning reduces double-merge risk — track that decision (Q2).
candidates.decision_rationale: Best-performing CRDT with a movable Tree + LWW-Map that maps onto entity-graph semantics, and Rust-core aligns with Flint's Rust fabric (likely same engine). Adopt as the ONE reference MergeStrategy impl behind a pluggable port — do not hard-wire.

candidates.name: @automerge/automerge

evidence.claim: v3.2.6, MIT, updated 2026-06-19 — healthy.
Automerge trades raw performance for a richer Git-like change-history model; ~85K weekly downloads.

risks: Slower than Loro in benchmarks; heavier history model than entity-graph LWW needs.
candidates.decision_rationale: Reference, not adopt. Its Git-like history is interesting for time-travel devtools (GAP-5) but overkill for entity merge. Keep as the second MergeStrategy impl if Flint picks Automerge.

candidates.name: yjs

evidence.claim: v13.6.31, MIT — the production default (~920K weekly downloads, 17K stars).
Largest ecosystem of bindings/providers; no-WASM (smaller bundle); second overall in benchmarks.

risks: Y.Map/Y.Array model is text/collab-document-oriented, not entity-graph-native — more impedance with our type:id:field shape than Loro's LWW-Map.
candidates.decision_rationale: Reference. Strongest ecosystem and bundle story, but its data model fits collaborative documents better than a normalized entity graph. The MergeStrategy port keeps the door open without committing.

candidates.name: @ag-ui/core + @ag-ui/client
candidates.fit_for_gap: GAP-4 Agent protocol ingestion (AG-UI / A2A / A2UI)

evidence.claim: v0.0.57, updated 2026-06-12 — actively developed but pre-1.0 (0.0.x), expect churn.
STATE_SNAPSHOT = full agent state; STATE_DELTA = partial update via JSON Patch (RFC 6902). 17 typed event types. AgentSubscriber consumption pattern with runtime validation + TS inference.
Adopted by Google, LangChain, AWS, Microsoft, Mastra, PydanticAI — the de-facto agent-UI state protocol.

risks: 0.0.x version — breaking changes likely; pin and isolate behind our own bridge module.
Keep @ag-ui/* an OPTIONAL peer dep so non-agentic users don't pay the bytes.
A2A / A2UI are separate from AG-UI — AG-UI covers agent→UI; A2A (Agent2Agent) is agent↔agent and is Flint's frf-agentproto concern, consumed via the Flint adapter, not this bridge.
candidates.decision_rationale: ADOPT for types/events, BUILD the thin bridge. STATE_DELTA is RFC-6902 JSON Patch and our patchEntity() is already JSON-Patch-shaped — applyAgUiDelta(graph, event) is a small, high-leverage module. Use @ag-ui/core for the typed event vocabulary; do not take a hard runtime dependency.

candidates.name: fast-json-patch
candidates.fit_for_gap: GAP-4 apply RFC-6902 JSON Patch deltas (AG-UI STATE_DELTA) to graph entities

evidence.claim: v3.1.1, MIT — stable but last release 2022 (mature/feature-complete, not abandoned-risk for a frozen RFC).
AG-UI STATE_DELTA is RFC-6902 — a standard, stable spec, so a mature applier is acceptable.

risks: Last release 2022 — for any non-trivial divergence prefer the applier already bundled in @ag-ui/client; evaluate both before adopting.
candidates.decision_rationale: Adopt as the RFC-6902 applier IF @ag-ui/client does not already expose one we can reuse. Tiny, standard, stable. Spec stage should confirm whether to reuse AG-UI's internal applier instead (preferred — one fewer dep).

candidates.name: @electric-sql/d2ts (differential dataflow for TS)

evidence.claim: v0.1.8, Apache-2.0, last release 2026-01-30 — very early (0.1.x), ElectricSQL-backed.
d2ts is the differential-dataflow engine powering TanStack DB's sub-ms incremental live queries; updating 1 row in a sorted 100k collection ≈ 0.7ms.

risks: 0.1.x — pre-production, API unstable; adopting now couples us to an early dependency.
Integrating differential dataflow under our existing view/evaluator is a deep architectural change, not a drop-in.
candidates.decision_rationale: Reference only for v2.0. The proven path TODAY is documented scale ceiling + @tanstack/react-virtual (already a dependency). Schedule a v2.x research spike to evaluate d2ts (or TanStack DB's live-query engine directly) once it stabilizes. Do not block v2.0 on it.

candidates.name: @tauri-apps/plugin-sql

evidence.claim: v2.4.0, MIT OR Apache-2.0, updated 2026-04-04 — official Tauri plugin, stable v2 line.
Flint targets Tauri (desktop) + web parity; on-device store is redb (Rust). SQLite via Tauri plugin is the natural JS-side persistence for the entity graph on desktop.

risks: Tauri-only — needs a parallel browser adapter (PGlite already covers browser/wasm).
candidates.decision_rationale: Adopt to implement a GraphPersistenceAdapter over Tauri SQL for desktop. Our GraphPersistenceAdapter interface (get/set/remove) already abstracts storage — this is a new adapter, not a core change. Pair with existing PGlite adapter for web.

candidates.name: wa-sqlite

evidence.claim: v1.0.0, last release 2024-01-05 — stable but we already ship a PGlite browser persistence adapter covering this need.

risks: Redundant with existing PGlite adapter; adds a second wasm-SQL engine for no clear gain.
candidates.decision_rationale: Reject. Browser SQL persistence is already covered by createPGlitePersistenceAdapter. No need for a second browser wasm engine. SQLite need is real only on Tauri/native — covered by cand-008.

candidates.name: TanStack DB (@tanstack/db)
candidates.fit_for_gap: GAP-3 incremental queries + competitive reference for normalized collections

evidence.claim: v0.6.9, MIT, updated 2026-06-19 — active; 1.0 targeted ~Dec 2025/early 2026.
Normalized in-memory collections + d2ts live queries with sub-ms incremental updates and cross-collection joins — the closest competitor to our model.

risks: TanStack-ecosystem-flavored; adopting wholesale would surrender our ecosystem-agnostic positioning (the moat).
candidates.decision_rationale: Reference, never adopt as a dependency. Study its live-query/collection ergonomics and d2ts integration to inform GAP-3, but our differentiator is transport-agnosticism + realtime-adapter breadth + AI surface, which TanStack DB does not have. Compete, don't consume.

candidates.name: Custom ESLint flat-config rule (no in-repo candidate)
candidates.fit_for_gap: GAP-7 Strict layering enforcement (no useGraphStore in component files)

evidence.claim: No off-the-shelf plugin enforces 'no direct store import in component files' for our specific Component→Hook→Store rule; this is project-specific architecture.

risks: A bespoke lint rule is maintenance we own; keep it minimal (one no-restricted-imports-style rule may suffice before writing a custom AST rule).
candidates.decision_rationale: Adapt: start with eslint no-restricted-imports (ban ./graph / useGraphStore in **/components/**, **/*.tsx that aren't hooks) before investing in a full custom plugin. Cheapest path to enforced layering. Promote to a published eslint-plugin-prometheus-entity only if the simple rule proves insufficient.

build_required.need: createFlintAdapter() — wrap Flint frf-entity-management RealtimeAdapter.watchEntities() AsyncIterable into our ChangeSet stream + map mutateEntity() to graph writes, with offset↔checkpoint resume.
build_required.why_no_candidate: The Flint SDK provides the spine transport (cand-001) but the translation into OUR RealtimeAdapter/ChangeSet contract + RealtimeManager coalescing is project-specific glue that must live in this repo.

build_required.need: MergeStrategy port — a pluggable seam at upsertEntity so conflict resolution (LWW default, Loro/Automerge/Yjs optional) is swappable per entity type.
build_required.why_no_candidate: CRDT libs (cand-002/003/004) provide the merge math but the integration seam wiring CRDT docs to our type/id/field entity model and syncMetadata.origin is bespoke.

build_required.need: applyAgUiDelta(graph, event) + applyAgUiSnapshot — bridge AG-UI STATE_DELTA (JSON Patch) / STATE_SNAPSHOT into the graph's patches/entities layers, mapping agent state paths to type:id:field.
build_required.why_no_candidate: AG-UI (cand-005) defines the events/types and JSON-Patch is appliable (cand-006), but the mapping of agent-state JSON paths onto our normalized entity graph is the novel, high-leverage integration.

build_required.need: Time-travel DevTools — wire the existing graph-actions action log + exportGraphSnapshot into the EntityExplorer panel as a timeline with state-before/after diff, snapshot import/export, and a graph-relationship visualization tab.
build_required.why_no_candidate: Redux/TanStack DevTools are not embeddable into our Zustand-graph-shaped panel; the raw materials (action log, snapshot export, devtools-event-bus) already exist in-repo and only need a new panel surface.

build_required.need: GraphPersistenceAdapter over @tauri-apps/plugin-sql (SQLite) for desktop on-device persistence.
build_required.why_no_candidate: cand-008 provides the SQL plugin; the adapter implementing our get/set/remove persistence contract over it is project glue.
